hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.loads(sys.argv[1]); print(d.get(\"tool_input\",{}).get(\"command\",\"\"))" "$INPUT"); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "git commit"; then MSG=$(git log -1 --pretty=%B 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$MSG" ]; then python3 -c "import sys,json; msg=sys.argv[1]; print(json.dumps({\"hookSpecificOutput\": {\"hookEventName\": \"PostToolUse\", \"additionalContext\": \"The user just made a git commit. The commit message is:\\n\\n\" + msg + \"\\n\\nPlease give feedback on this commit message to help improve their commit message writing skills. Consider: clarity, use of imperative mood, appropriate length, whether it explains the why not just the what, and other best practices.\"}}})" "$MSG"; fi; fi'
